g.text: Great topic. For affordability, start with these core datasets (all available in RESEARCH):
- Census ACS 5‑year housing/economic tables (tract/county): median gross rent, median household income, owner costs, cos
- HUD Fair Market Rents and Income Limits (county/metro): program-standard rents and AMI tiers.
- Zillow ZORI/ZHVI (ZIP/county/metro, monthly): current rent/home value trends to complement slower ACS.
- BLS CPI (Shelter index): to deflate rents/incomes to real terms.
- Base: ACS 5‑year to compute affordability (e.g., rent-to-income ratios; share paying >30% income).
- Add: Zillow ZORI for recent monthly movement.
- Optional: HUD Income Limits to express results vs 60%/80%/100% AMI.
- Geography (nationwide, state, metro, county, tract?) and time span.
- Whether you prefer renter focus, owner focus, or both.
I’ll then check your existing research environments, reuse one if it matches, or create one that fetches ACS (latest 2 v
intages) + ZORI, and set up ready-to-query tables for affordability metrics.
